text: Machine learning teaches computers to learn from data. In supervised learning, we give the computer training data that has correct answers. For example, we might show the machine many photos of cats and dogs, with each photo labelled correctly. The computer uses this data to build a model. A model is like a set of rules that helps the machine make predictions. Once the model is ready, we can test it with new photos. Its accuracy shows how often its predictions are correct. Better training data usually leads to higher accuracy. That is how machines learn to recognize patterns without being programmed for every scenario.
